steps.title: Calculate the mean of data set X
steps.content:
Add the four values: $5 + 9 + 9 + 13 = 36$.
Divide by the count: $\frac{36}{4} = 9$.
Mean of X is \$9$.

steps.title: Calculate the mean of data set Y
steps.content:
Data set Y has the same first four values as X, plus an additional value \$27$, so the sum is $36 + 27 = 63$.
Divide by the count: $\frac{63}{5} = 12.6$.
Mean of Y is \$12.6$.

steps.title: Compare the means
steps.content:
Mean of X is \$9$ and mean of Y is \$12.6$.
Since \$9 < 12.6$, the mean of data set X is less than the mean of data set Y. That matches choice B — B is correct.
